    18‚Üíimport Mathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.Bipartite
    19‚Üíimport Mathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.Matching
    20‚Üíimport Mathlib.Data.Real.Archimedean

    23‚Üínamespace SimpleGraph
    24‚Üí
    25‚Üíopen Classical Finset List
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üívariable {őĪ : Type*} [Fintype őĪ] [DecidableEq őĪ]
    28‚Üí
    29‚Üí/-- `Ls G` is the maximum number of leaves over all spanning trees of `G`.
    30‚ÜíIt is defined to be `0` when `G` is not connected. -/
    31‚Üínoncomputable def Ls (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚ĄĚ :=
    32‚Üí  letI spanningTrees := { T : Subgraph G | T.IsSpanning ‚ąß IsTree T.coe }
    33‚Üí  letI leaves (T : Subgraph G) := T.verts.toFinset.filter (fun v => T.degree v = 1)
    34‚Üí  letI num_leaves (T : Subgraph G) := (leaves T).card
    35‚Üí  sSup (Set.image (fun T => (num_leaves T : ‚ĄĚ)) spanningTrees)
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí/-- `n G` is the number of vertices of `G` as a real number. -/
    38‚Üínoncomputable def n (_ : SimpleGraph őĪ) : ‚ĄĚ := Fintype.card őĪ
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üí/-- `m G` is the size of a maximum matching of `G`. -/
    41‚Üínoncomputable def m (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚ĄĚ :=
    42‚Üí  let matchings := { M : Subgraph G | M.IsMatching }
    43‚Üí  sSup (Set.image (fun M => (M.edgeSet.toFinset.card : ‚ĄĚ)) matchings)
    44‚Üí
    45‚Üí/-- The maximum cardinality among all independent sets `s`
    46‚Üí    that maximize the quantity `|s| - |N(s)|`, where `N(s)`
    47‚Üí    is the neighborhood of the set `s`. -/
    48‚Üínoncomputable def aprime (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚ĄĚ :=
    49‚Üí  letI indep_sets : Finset (Finset őĪ) := univ.powerset.filter (fun s => G.IsIndepSet (s : Set őĪ))
    50‚Üí  letI diff (s : Finset őĪ) : ‚Ą§ := (s.card : ‚Ą§) - (‚čÉ v ‚ąą (s : Set őĪ), G.neighborSet v).toFinset.card
    51‚Üí  letI max_diff := (indep_sets.image diff).max
    52‚Üí  letI critical_sets := indep_sets.filter (fun s ‚Ü¶ diff s = max_diff.getD 0)
    53‚Üí  letI max_card := (critical_sets.image Finset.card).max
    54‚Üí  (max_card.getD 0 : ‚ĄĚ)
    55‚Üí
    56‚Üí/-- `largestInducedForestSize G` is the size of a largest induced forest of `G`. -/
    57‚Üínoncomputable def largestInducedForestSize (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) : ‚Ąē :=
    58‚Üí  sSup { n | ‚ąÉ s : Finset őĪ, (G.induce s).IsAcyclic ‚ąß s.card = n }
    59‚Üí
    60‚Üí/-- The degree sequence of a graph, sorted in nondecreasing order. -/
    61‚Üínoncomputable def degreeSequence (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : List ‚Ąē :=
    62‚Üí  (Finset.univ.val.map fun v : őĪ => G.degree v).sort (¬∑ ‚Č§ ¬∑)
    63‚Üí
    64‚Üí/--
    65‚ÜíThe maximum number of occurrences of any term of the degree sequence of `G`.
    66‚Üí-/
    67‚Üínoncomputable def degreeSequenceMultiplicity (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚Ąē :=
    68‚Üí  letI degs := degreeSequence G
    69‚Üí  (List.max? (degs.map (fun d => degs.count d))).getD 0
    70‚Üí
    71‚Üí/-- `largestInducedBipartiteSubgraphSize G` is the size of a largest induced
    72‚Üíbipartite subgraph of `G`. -/
    73‚Üínoncomputable def largestInducedBipartiteSubgraphSize (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) : ‚Ąē :=
    74‚Üí  sSup { n | ‚ąÉ s : Finset őĪ, (G.induce s).IsBipartite ‚ąß s.card = n }
    75‚Üí
    76‚Üí/-- `b G` is the number of vertices of a largest induced bipartite subgraph of `G`.
    77‚ÜíReturned as a real number. -/
    78‚Üínoncomputable def b (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) : ‚ĄĚ :=
    79‚Üí  (largestInducedBipartiteSubgraphSize G : ‚ĄĚ)
    80‚Üí
    81‚Üí/-- Independence number of the neighbourhood of `v`. -/
    82‚Üínoncomputable def indepNeighborsCard (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) (v : őĪ) : ‚Ąē :=
    83‚Üí  (G.induce (G.neighborSet v)).indepNum
    84‚Üí
    85‚Üí/-- The same quantity as a real number. -/
    86‚Üínoncomputable def indepNeighbors (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) (v : őĪ) : ‚ĄĚ :=
    87‚Üí  (indepNeighborsCard G v : ‚ĄĚ)
    88‚Üí
    89‚Üí/-- Average of `indepNeighbors` over all vertices. -/
    90‚Üínoncomputable def averageIndepNeighbors (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) : ‚ĄĚ :=
    91‚Üí  (‚ąĎ v ‚ąą Finset.univ, indepNeighbors G v) / (Fintype.card őĪ : ‚ĄĚ)
    92‚Üí
    93‚Üí/-- A unit distance graph in ‚ĄĚ¬≤:
    94‚ÜíA graph where the vertices V are a collection of points in ‚ĄĚ¬≤ and there is
    95‚Üían edge between two points if and only if the distance between them is 1. -/
    96‚Üídef UnitDistancePlaneGraph (V : Set (EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 2))) : SimpleGraph V where
    97‚Üí  Adj x y := Dist.dist x y = 1
    98‚Üí  symm x y := by simp [PseudoMetricSpace.dist_comm]
    99‚Üí
   100‚Üí/--
   101‚ÜíTwo walks are internally disjoint if they share no vertices other than their endpoints.
   102‚Üí-/
   103‚Üídef InternallyDisjoint {V : Type*} {G : SimpleGraph V} {u v x y : V}
   104‚Üí    (p : G.Walk u v) (q : G.Walk x y) : Prop :=
   105‚Üí  Disjoint p.support.tail.dropLast q.support.tail.dropLast
   106‚Üí
   107‚Üí/--
   108‚ÜíWe say a graph is infinitely connected if any two vertices are connected by infinitely many
   109‚Üípairwise disjoint paths.
   110‚Üí-/
   111‚Üídef InfinitelyConnected {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) : Prop :=
   112‚Üí  Pairwise fun u v ‚Ü¶ ‚ąÉ P : Set (G.Walk u v),
   113‚Üí    P.Infinite ‚ąß (‚ąÄ p ‚ąą P, p.IsPath) ‚ąß P.Pairwise InternallyDisjoint
   114‚Üí
   115‚Üí/-- Infinite graphs: definitions for max degree and clique number so that the maximum
   116‚Üídegree (resp. clique number) of a graph with unbounded degree (resp. clique size) is
   117‚Üí`‚ąě` rather than 0.
   118‚Üí-/
   119‚Üínoncomputable
   120‚Üídef edegree {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) (v : V) : ‚Ąē‚ąě := (G.neighborSet v).encard
   121‚Üí
   122‚Üínoncomputable
   123‚Üídef emaxDegree {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) : ‚Ąē‚ąě := ‚®Ü v, G.edegree v
   124‚Üí
   125‚Üínoncomputable
   126‚Üídef ecliqueNum {V : Type} (G : SimpleGraph V) : ‚Ąē‚ąě := ‚®Ü (s : Finset V) (_ : G.IsClique s), #s
   127‚Üí
   128‚Üíend SimpleGraph
   129‚Üí

    16‚Üíimport FormalConjecturesForMathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.Clique

    21‚Üí
    22‚Üívariable {V őĪ őĻ : Type*} {G : SimpleGraph V} {n : ‚Ąē}
    23‚Üí
    24‚Üínamespace SimpleGraph
    25‚Üí
    26‚Üí@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "Ōá(" G ")" => chromaticNumber G
    27‚Üí
    28‚Üílemma le_chromaticNumber_iff_colorable : n ‚Č§ G.chromaticNumber ‚ÜĒ ‚ąÄ m, G.Colorable m ‚Üí n ‚Č§ m := by
    29‚Üí  simp [chromaticNumber]
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üílemma le_chromaticNumber_iff_coloring :
    32‚Üí    n ‚Č§ G.chromaticNumber ‚ÜĒ ‚ąÄ m, G.Coloring (Fin m) ‚Üí n ‚Č§ m := by
    33‚Üí  simp [le_chromaticNumber_iff_colorable, Colorable]
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üílemma lt_chromaticNumber_iff_not_colorable : n < G.chromaticNumber ‚ÜĒ ¬¨ G.Colorable n := by
    36‚Üí  rw [‚Üź chromaticNumber_le_iff_colorable, not_le]
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üílemma le_chromaticNumber_iff_not_colorable (hn : n ‚Č† 0) :
    39‚Üí    n ‚Č§ G.chromaticNumber ‚ÜĒ ¬¨ G.Colorable (n - 1) := by
    40‚Üí  let n + 1 := n; simp [ENat.add_one_le_iff, lt_chromaticNumber_iff_not_colorable]
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üílemma Coloring.injective_comp_of_pairwise_adj (C : G.Coloring őĪ) (f : őĻ ‚Üí V)
    43‚Üí    (hf : Pairwise fun i j ‚Ü¶ G.Adj (f i) (f j)) : (C ‚ąė f).Injective :=
    44‚Üí  Function.injective_iff_pairwise_ne.2 fun _i _j hij ‚Ü¶ C.valid <| hf hij
    45‚Üí
    46‚Üílemma Colorable.card_le_of_pairwise_adj (hG : G.Colorable n) (f : őĻ ‚Üí V)
    47‚Üí    (hf : Pairwise fun i j ‚Ü¶ G.Adj (f i) (f j)) : Nat.card őĻ ‚Č§ n := by
    48‚Üí  obtain ‚ü®C‚ü© := hG
    49‚Üí  simpa using Nat.card_le_card_of_injective _ (C.injective_comp_of_pairwise_adj f hf)
    50‚Üí
    51‚Üílemma le_chromaticNumber_of_pairwise_adj (hn : n ‚Č§ Nat.card őĻ) (f : őĻ ‚Üí V)
    52‚Üí    (hf : Pairwise fun i j ‚Ü¶ G.Adj (f i) (f j)) : n ‚Č§ G.chromaticNumber :=
    53‚Üí  le_chromaticNumber_iff_colorable.2 fun _m hm ‚Ü¶ hn.trans <| hm.card_le_of_pairwise_adj f hf
    54‚Üí
    55‚Üílemma card_div_indepNum_le_chromaticNumber : ‚Ćą(Nat.card V / őĪ(G) : ‚Ąö‚Č•0)‚ĆČ‚āä ‚Č§ G.chromaticNumber := by
    56‚Üí  cases finite_or_infinite V
    57‚Üí  swap; ¬∑ simp
    58‚Üí  cases nonempty_fintype V
    59‚Üí  simp only [Nat.card_eq_fintype_card, le_chromaticNumber_iff_coloring, Nat.ceil_le]
    60‚Üí  refine fun m c ‚Ü¶ div_le_of_le_mul‚āÄ (by simp) (by simp) ?_
    61‚Üí  norm_cast
    62‚Üí  rw [‚Üź mul_one (Fintype.card V), ‚Üź Fintype.card_fin m]
    63‚Üí  refine Finset.card_mul_le_card_mul (c ¬∑ = ¬∑)
    64‚Üí    (by simp [Finset.bipartiteAbove, Finset.filter_nonempty_iff])
    65‚Üí    fun b _ ‚Ü¶ IsIndepSet.card_le_indepNum ?_
    66‚Üí  simpa [IsIndepSet, Set.Pairwise] using fun x hx y hy _ ‚Ü¶ c.not_adj_of_mem_colorClass hx hy
    67‚Üí
    68‚Üíinstance (f : őĻ ‚Üí V) : Std.Symm fun i j ‚Ü¶ G.Adj (f i) (f j) where symm _ _ := .symm
    69‚Üí
    70‚Üívariable (G) in
    71‚Üí/-- A set of edges is critical if deleting them reduces the chromatic number. -/
    72‚Üídef IsCriticalEdges (edges : Set (Sym2 V)) : Prop :=
    73‚Üí  (G.deleteEdges edges).chromaticNumber < G.chromaticNumber
    74‚Üí
    75‚Üívariable (G) in
    76‚Üí/-- An edge is critical if deleting it reduces the chromatic number. -/
    77‚Üídef IsCriticalEdge (e : Sym2 V) : Prop := G.IsCriticalEdges ({e} : Set (Sym2 V))
    78‚Üí
    79‚Üí/--
    80‚ÜíA set of vertices is critical if deleting them reduces the chromatic number.
    81‚Üí-/
    82‚Üídef Subgraph.IsCriticalVerts (verts : Set V) (G' : G.Subgraph) : Prop :=
    83‚Üí  (G'.deleteVerts verts).coe.chromaticNumber < G'.coe.chromaticNumber
    84‚Üí
    85‚Üí/--
    86‚ÜíA vertex is critical if deleting it reduces the chromatic number.

    88‚Üídef Subgraph.IsCriticalVertex (v : V) (G' : G.Subgraph) : Prop := G'.IsCriticalVerts {v}
    89‚Üí
    90‚Üívariable (G)
    91‚Üí
    92‚Üí/--
    93‚ÜíA graph `G` is `k`-critical (or vertex-critical) if its chromatic number is `k`,
    94‚Üíand deleting any single vertex reduces the chromatic number.
    95‚Üí-/
    96‚Üídef IsCritical (k : ‚Ąē) : Prop := G.chromaticNumber = k ‚ąß ‚ąÄ v, (‚ä§ : G.Subgraph).IsCriticalVertex v
    97‚Üí
    98‚Üítheorem not_isCritical_of_fintype_lt [Fintype V] (k : ‚Ąē) (hk : Fintype.card V < k) :
    99‚Üí   ¬¨G.IsCritical k := by
   100‚Üí  simp [IsCritical]
   101‚Üí  intro h
   102‚Üí  have := h ‚Ėł SimpleGraph.chromaticNumber_le_iff_colorable.2 G.colorable_of_fintype
   103‚Üí  simp at this
   104‚Üí  grind
   105‚Üí
   106‚Üíopen SimpleGraph
   107‚Üí
   108‚Üítheorem colorable_iff_induce_eq_bot (G : SimpleGraph V) (n : ‚Ąē) :
   109‚Üí    G.Colorable n ‚ÜĒ ‚ąÉ coloring : V ‚Üí Fin n, ‚ąÄ i, G.induce {v | coloring v = i} = ‚ä• := by
   110‚Üí  refine ‚ü®fun ‚ü®a, h‚ü© ‚Ü¶ ‚ü®a, by aesop‚ü©, fun ‚ü®w, h‚ü© ‚Ü¶ ‚ü®w, @fun a b h_adj ‚Ü¶ ?_‚ü©‚ü©
   111‚Üí  specialize h (w a)
   112‚Üí  contrapose h
   113‚Üí  intro hG
   114‚Üí  have : ¬¨ ((SimpleGraph.induce {v | w v = w a} G).Adj ‚ü®a, by rfl‚ü© ‚ü®b, by simp_all‚ü©) :=
   115‚Üí    hG ‚Ėł fun a ‚Ü¶ a
   116‚Üí  exact this h_adj
   117‚Üí
   118‚Üídef Cocolorable (G : SimpleGraph V) (n : ‚Ąē) : Prop := ‚ąÉ coloring : V ‚Üí Fin n,
   119‚Üí  ‚ąÄ i, G.induce {v | coloring v = i} = ‚ä• ‚ą® G.induce {v | coloring v = i} = ‚ä§
   120‚Üí
   121‚Üíexample (G : SimpleGraph V) (n : ‚Ąē) : G.Colorable n ‚Üí SimpleGraph.Cocolorable G n := by
   122‚Üí  simp [colorable_iff_induce_eq_bot, Cocolorable]
   123‚Üí  aesop
   124‚Üí
   125‚Üí/--
   126‚ÜíThe chromatic number of a graph is the minimal number of colors needed to color it.
   127‚ÜíThis is `‚ä§` (infinity) iff `G` isn't colorable with finitely many colors.
   128‚Üí
   129‚ÜíIf `G` is colorable, then `ENat.toNat G.chromaticNumber` is the `‚Ąē`-valued chromatic number.
   130‚Üí-/
   131‚Üínoncomputable def cochromaticNumber (G : SimpleGraph V) : ‚Ąē‚ąě := ‚®Ö n ‚ąą setOf G.Cocolorable, (n : ‚Ąē‚ąě)
   132‚Üí
   133‚Üí/-- The chromatic cardinal is the minimal number of colors need to color it. In contrast to
   134‚Üí`chromaticNumber`, which assigns `‚ä§ : ‚Ąē‚ąě` to all non-finitely colorable graphs, this definition
   135‚Üíreturns a `Cardinal` and can therefore distinguish between different infinite chromatic numbers. -/
   136‚Üínoncomputable def chromaticCardinal.{u} {V : Type u} (G : SimpleGraph V) : Cardinal :=
   137‚Üí  sInf {őļ : Cardinal | ‚ąÉ (C : Type u) (_ : Cardinal.mk C = őļ), Nonempty (G.Coloring C)}
   138‚Üí
   139‚Üí/-- The maximum size of the union of k finite independent sets. -/
   140‚Üínoncomputable def indepNumK (G : SimpleGraph V) (k : ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē :=
   141‚Üí  sSup {n | ‚ąÉ f : Fin k ‚Üí Set V, (‚ąÄ i, G.IsIndepSet (f i)) ‚ąß (‚čÉ i, f i).ncard = n}
   142‚Üí
   143‚Üí/-- A finite graph is CDS-colorable if it has a proper coloring
   144‚Üíby natural numbers such that for all `k > 0`, the number of
   145‚Üívertices with color `< k` equals the maximum size of
   146‚Üíthe union of `k` independent sets. -/
   147‚Üídef CDSColorable [Fintype őĪ] {G : SimpleGraph őĪ} : Prop :=
   148‚Üí    ‚ąÉ (C : G.Coloring Nat), ‚ąÄ k : Nat,
   149‚Üí   ‚ąĎ i < k, (C.colorClass i).ncard = indepNumK G k
   150‚Üí
   151‚Üí/-- A homomorphism is rainbow if it maps distinct edges to distinct colors. -/
   152‚Üídef IsRainbow {őĪ V : Type*} {H : SimpleGraph őĪ} {G : SimpleGraph V} (f : H ‚Üíg G) {C : Type*}
   153‚Üí    (c : Sym2 V ‚Üí C) : Prop :=
   154‚Üí  Function.Injective fun e : H.edgeSet => c (Sym2.map f e)
   155‚Üí
   156‚Üí/--
   157‚ÜíThe anti-Ramsey number $\mathrm{AR}(n, H)$: maximum colors to edge-color $K_n$ without rainbow $H$.
   158‚Üí-/
   159‚Üínoncomputable def antiRamseyNum {őĪ : Type*} [Fintype őĪ] (H : SimpleGraph őĪ) (n : ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē :=
   160‚Üí  sSup {k | ‚ąÉ c : Sym2 (Fin n) ‚Üí Fin k, ‚ąÄ f : H ‚Üíg ‚ä§, ¬¨IsRainbow f c}

    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 797
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/797](https://www.erdosproblems.com/797)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíLet `f(d)` be the maximal acyclic chromatic number of any graph with maximum degree `d`.
    25‚ÜíEstimate `f(d)`. In particular, is it true that `f(d) = o(d¬≤)`?
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üí[AlBe76] Albertson, M. O. and Berman, D. M., *Every planar graph has an acyclic
    28‚Üí7-coloring*, 1976.
    29‚Üí
    30‚Üí[AMR91] Alon, N., McDiarmid, C., and Reed, B., *Acyclic coloring of graphs*, 1991.
    31‚Üí-/
    32‚Üí
    33‚Üíopen SimpleGraph Real Classical
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üínamespace Erdos797
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí/-- A proper coloring of $G$ is acyclic if for every pair of distinct colors,
    38‚Üí    the subgraph induced by vertices of those two colors is acyclic (a forest). -/
    39‚Üídef IsAcyclicColoring {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) {őĪ : Type*}
    40‚Üí    (c : G.Coloring őĪ) : Prop :=
    41‚Üí  ‚ąÄ a b : őĪ, a ‚Č† b ‚Üí
    42‚Üí    (G.induce {v : V | c v = a ‚ą® c v = b}).IsAcyclic
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üí/--
    45‚ÜíLet $f(d)$ be the maximal acyclic chromatic number of any graph with maximum
    46‚Üídegree $d$. Alon, McDiarmid, and Reed [AMR91] showed
    47‚Üí$$d^{4/3} / (\log d)^{1/3} \ll f(d) \ll d^{4/3}.$$
    48‚Üí-/
    49‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    50‚Üítheorem erdos_797 :
    51‚Üí    -- Upper bound: f(d) ‚Č™ d^{4/3}
    52‚Üí    (‚ąÉ C : ‚ĄĚ, 0 < C ‚ąß
    53‚Üí      ‚ąÄ (d n : ‚Ąē) (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)),
    54‚Üí        (‚ąÄ v, G.degree v ‚Č§ d) ‚Üí
    55‚Üí        ‚ąÉ (k : ‚Ąē) (c : G.Coloring (Fin k)),
    56‚Üí          IsAcyclicColoring G c ‚ąß (k : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č§ C * (d : ‚ĄĚ) ^ ((4 : ‚ĄĚ) / 3)) ‚ąß
    57‚Üí    -- Lower bound: f(d) ‚Čę d^{4/3} / (log d)^{1/3}
    58‚Üí    (‚ąÉ C : ‚ĄĚ, 0 < C ‚ąß
    59‚Üí      ‚ąÉ d‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ d : ‚Ąē, d‚āÄ ‚Č§ d ‚Üí
    60‚Üí        ‚ąÉ (n : ‚Ąē) (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)),
    61‚Üí          (‚ąÄ v, G.degree v ‚Č§ d) ‚ąß
    62‚Üí          ‚ąÄ (k : ‚Ąē), (‚ąÉ c : G.Coloring (Fin k), IsAcyclicColoring G c) ‚Üí
    63‚Üí            C * (d : ‚ĄĚ) ^ ((4 : ‚ĄĚ) / 3) / (Real.log (d : ‚ĄĚ)) ^ ((1 : ‚ĄĚ) / 3) ‚Č§ (k : ‚ĄĚ)) := by
    64‚Üí  sorry
    65‚Üí
    66‚Üíend Erdos797
    67‚Üí

    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 993
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/993](https://www.erdosproblems.com/993)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíA question of Alavi, Malde, Schwenk, and ErdŇĎs [AMSE87], who showed that the independent set
    25‚Üísequence is not constrained for general graphs (in fact every possible pattern of inequalities
    26‚Üíis achieved by some graph).
    27‚Üí
    28‚Üí[AMSE87] Alavi, Y., Malde, P. J., Schwenk, A. J., and ErdŇĎs, P., *The vertex independence
    29‚Üísequence of a graph is not constrained*. Congressus Numerantium (1987), 58, 15-23.
    30‚Üí-/
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üíopen SimpleGraph
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üínamespace Erdos993
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üí/-- The number of independent sets of size $k$ in a simple graph $G$.
    37‚ÜíAn independent set is a set of vertices that are pairwise non-adjacent. -/
    38‚Üídef numIndepSets {V : Type*} [Fintype V] [DecidableEq V]
    39‚Üí    (G : SimpleGraph V) [DecidableRel G.Adj] (k : ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē :=
    40‚Üí  (Finset.univ.powerset.filter (fun s : Finset V =>
    41‚Üí    s.card = k ‚ąß ‚ąÄ ‚¶Éu‚¶Ą, u ‚ąą s ‚Üí ‚ąÄ ‚¶Év‚¶Ą, v ‚ąą s ‚Üí u ‚Č† v ‚Üí ¬¨G.Adj u v)).card
    42‚Üí
    43‚Üí/--
    44‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 993 [AMSE87]:
    45‚Üí
    46‚ÜíThe independent set sequence of any tree or forest is unimodal. That is, if
    47‚Üí$i_k(T)$ counts the number of independent sets of vertices of size $k$ in a
    48‚Üíforest $T$, then there exists $m \geq 0$ such that
    49‚Üí$$
    50‚Üíi_0(T) \leq i_1(T) \leq \cdots \leq i_m(T) \geq i_{m+1}(T) \geq i_{m+2}(T) \geq \cdots
    51‚Üí$$
    52‚Üí-/
    53‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5]
    54‚Üítheorem erdos_993 {V : Type*} [Fintype V] [DecidableEq V]
    55‚Üí    (T : SimpleGraph V) [DecidableRel T.Adj] (hT : T.IsAcyclic) :
    56‚Üí    ‚ąÉ m : ‚Ąē, (‚ąÄ i j : ‚Ąē, i ‚Č§ j ‚Üí j ‚Č§ m ‚Üí numIndepSets T i ‚Č§ numIndepSets T j) ‚ąß
    57‚Üí             (‚ąÄ i j : ‚Ąē, m ‚Č§ i ‚Üí i ‚Č§ j ‚Üí numIndepSets T j ‚Č§ numIndepSets T i) := by
    58‚Üí  sorry
    59‚Üí
    60‚Üíend Erdos993
    61‚Üí

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 761
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/761](https://www.erdosproblems.com/761)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíThe cochromatic number of $G$, denoted by $\zeta(G)$, is the minimum number of colours
    25‚Üíneeded to colour the vertices of $G$ such that each colour class induces either
    26‚Üía complete graph or empty graph. The dichromatic number of $G$, denoted by $\delta(G)$,
    27‚Üíis the minimum number $k$ of colours required such that, in any orientation of
    28‚Üíthe edges of $G$, there is a $k$-colouring of the vertices of $G$ such that there
    29‚Üíare no monochromatic oriented cycles.
    30‚Üí
    31‚ÜíThe first question is due to ErdŇĎs and Neumann-Lara. The second question is
    32‚Üídue to ErdŇĎs and Gimbel. A positive answer to the second question implies a
    33‚Üípositive answer to the first via the bound mentioned in Problem 760.
    34‚Üí-/
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üíopen SimpleGraph
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üínamespace Erdos761
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üí/-- An orientation of a simple graph assigns a direction to each edge:
    41‚Üíeach edge $\{u,v\}$ is oriented as $u \to v$ or $v \to u$ (but not both). -/
    42‚Üídef IsOrientation {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) (D : V ‚Üí V ‚Üí Prop) : Prop :=
    43‚Üí  (‚ąÄ u v, D u v ‚Üí G.Adj u v) ‚ąß
    44‚Üí  (‚ąÄ u v, G.Adj u v ‚Üí D u v ‚ą® D v u) ‚ąß
    45‚Üí  (‚ąÄ u v, D u v ‚Üí ¬¨D v u)
    46‚Üí
    47‚Üí/-- A colouring $c : V \to \operatorname{Fin}(k)$ is acyclic with respect to an orientation $D$
    48‚Üíif there is no monochromatic directed cycle: no vertex can reach itself
    49‚Üívia directed edges within its own colour class. -/
    50‚Üídef IsAcyclicColouring {V : Type*} (D : V ‚Üí V ‚Üí Prop) (k : ‚Ąē)
    51‚Üí    (c : V ‚Üí Fin k) : Prop :=
    52‚Üí  ‚ąÄ i : Fin k, ‚ąÄ v : V, c v = i ‚Üí
    53‚Üí    ¬¨Relation.TransGen (fun u w => c u = i ‚ąß c w = i ‚ąß D u w) v v
    54‚Üí
    55‚Üí/-- The dichromatic number of $G$: the minimum $k$ such that for every orientation
    56‚Üíof $G$, there exists an acyclic $k$-colouring. -/
    57‚Üínoncomputable def dichromaticNumber {V : Type*} [Fintype V]
    58‚Üí    (G : SimpleGraph V) : ‚Ąē :=
    59‚Üí  sInf {k : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÄ D : V ‚Üí V ‚Üí Prop, IsOrientation G D ‚Üí
    60‚Üí    ‚ąÉ c : V ‚Üí Fin k, IsAcyclicColouring D k c}
    61‚Üí
    62‚Üí/-- A cochromatic colouring: each colour class induces either a complete
    63‚Üísubgraph or an independent set. -/
    64‚Üídef IsCochromaticColouring {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) (k : ‚Ąē)
    65‚Üí    (c : V ‚Üí Fin k) : Prop :=
    66‚Üí  ‚ąÄ i : Fin k,
    67‚Üí    (‚ąÄ u v, c u = i ‚Üí c v = i ‚Üí u ‚Č† v ‚Üí G.Adj u v) ‚ą®
    68‚Üí    (‚ąÄ u v, c u = i ‚Üí c v = i ‚Üí u ‚Č† v ‚Üí ¬¨G.Adj u v)
    69‚Üí
    70‚Üí/-- The cochromatic number $\zeta(G)$: minimum number of colours in a cochromatic
    71‚Üícolouring. -/
    72‚Üínoncomputable def cochromaticNumber {V : Type*} [Fintype V]
    73‚Üí    (G : SimpleGraph V) : ‚Ąē :=
    74‚Üí  sInf {k : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÉ c : V ‚Üí Fin k, IsCochromaticColouring G k c}
    75‚Üí
    76‚Üí/--
    77‚Üí**ErdŇĎs Problem 761, Question 1** (ErdŇĎs‚ÄďNeumann-Lara):
    78‚Üí
    79‚ÜíMust a graph with large chromatic number have large dichromatic number?
    80‚ÜíFor every $n$ there exists $m$ such that $\chi(G) \geq m$ implies $\delta(G) \geq n$.
    81‚Üí-/
    82‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5]
    83‚Üítheorem erdos_761 : answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ
    84‚Üí    (‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÉ m : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ (s : ‚Ąē) (G : SimpleGraph (Fin s)),
    85‚Üí      G.chromaticNumber.toNat ‚Č• m ‚Üí
    86‚Üí        dichromaticNumber G ‚Č• n) := by
    87‚Üí  sorry
    88‚Üí
    89‚Üí/--
    90‚Üí**ErdŇĎs Problem 761, Question 2** (ErdŇĎs‚ÄďGimbel):
    91‚Üí
    92‚ÜíMust a graph with large cochromatic number contain a subgraph with large
    93‚Üídichromatic number? For every $n$ there exists $m$ such that $\zeta(G) \geq m$ implies
    94‚Üí$G$ contains an induced subgraph with $\delta \geq n$.
    95‚Üí-/
    96‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5]
    97‚Üítheorem erdos_761.variants.cochromatic : answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ
    98‚Üí    (‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÉ m : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ (s : ‚Ąē) (G : SimpleGraph (Fin s)),
    99‚Üí      cochromaticNumber G ‚Č• m ‚Üí
   100‚Üí        ‚ąÉ (S : Finset (Fin s)),
   101‚Üí          dichromaticNumber (G.induce (‚ÜĎS : Set (Fin s))) ‚Č• n) := by
   102‚Üí  sorry
   103‚Üí
   104‚Üíend Erdos761
   105‚Üí

    24‚ÜíLet $G$ be a graph with girth $> 4$. Can the edges of $G$ always be oriented so that the
    25‚Üíresulting directed graph is acyclic, and reversing any single edge also leaves it acyclic?

    33‚Üíhypergraphs without short cycles*, 1978.
    34‚Üí-/
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üíopen SimpleGraph

    38‚Üínamespace Erdos1006
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üívariable {V : Type*}
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üí/-- An orientation of a simple graph $G$: a relation `dir` on $V$ such that for each
    43‚Üíedge $\{u, v\}$, exactly one of `dir u v` or `dir v u` holds, and `dir` only
    44‚Üírelates adjacent vertices. -/
    45‚Üístructure Orientation (G : SimpleGraph V) where
    46‚Üí  dir : V ‚Üí V ‚Üí Prop
    47‚Üí  adj_of_dir : ‚ąÄ u v, dir u v ‚Üí G.Adj u v
    48‚Üí  dir_of_adj : ‚ąÄ u v, G.Adj u v ‚Üí dir u v ‚ą® dir v u
    49‚Üí  antisymm : ‚ąÄ u v, dir u v ‚Üí ¬¨dir v u
    50‚Üí
    51‚Üí/-- An orientation is acyclic if the transitive closure of its direction relation
    52‚Üíis irreflexive (equivalently, there are no directed cycles). -/
    53‚Üídef Orientation.IsAcyclic {G : SimpleGraph V} (o : Orientation G) : Prop :=
    54‚Üí  ‚ąÄ v, ¬¨Relation.TransGen o.dir v v
    55‚Üí
    56‚Üí/-- The relation obtained by flipping one directed edge $(a \to b)$ to $(b \to a)$,
    57‚Üíkeeping all other directed edges the same. -/
    58‚Üídef flipDir (dir : V ‚Üí V ‚Üí Prop) (a b : V) : V ‚Üí V ‚Üí Prop :=
    59‚Üí  fun u v => (u = b ‚ąß v = a ‚ąß dir a b) ‚ą® (dir u v ‚ąß ¬¨(u = a ‚ąß v = b))
    60‚Üí
    61‚Üí/-- An orientation is robustly acyclic if it is acyclic and reversing any
    62‚Üísingle directed edge also yields an acyclic relation. -/
    63‚Üídef Orientation.IsRobustlyAcyclic {G : SimpleGraph V} (o : Orientation G) : Prop :=
    64‚Üí  o.IsAcyclic ‚ąß
    65‚Üí  ‚ąÄ a b, o.dir a b ‚Üí
    66‚Üí    ‚ąÄ v, ¬¨Relation.TransGen (flipDir o.dir a b) v v
    67‚Üí
    68‚Üí/--

    70‚Üí
    71‚ÜíLet $G$ be a graph with girth $> 4$ (i.e., $G$ contains no cycles of length $3$ or $4$).
    72‚ÜíCan the edges of $G$ always be directed such that there is no directed cycle, and
    73‚Üíreversing the direction of any edge also creates no directed cycle?
    74‚Üí
    75‚ÜíThis was disproved by NeŇ°etŇôil and R√∂dl [NeRo78b], who proved that for every
    76‚Üíinteger $g$, there is a graph $G$ with girth $g$ such that every orientation of $G$ either
    77‚Üícontains a directed cycle or contains a cycle obtained by reversing one edge.
    78‚Üí-/

    82‚Üí    ‚ąÉ o : Orientation G, o.IsRobustlyAcyclic := by

    85‚Üíend Erdos1006

    27‚Üívalue of $\zeta(G)$ over all graphs $G$ with $n$ vertices.
    28‚Üí
    29‚Üí[ErGi93] ErdŇĎs, P. and Gimbel, J., *Some problems and results in cochromatic theory* (1993).
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üí[Gi86] Gimbel, J., *The chromatic and cochromatic number of a graph* (1986).
    32‚Üí-/
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üíopen SimpleGraph
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üínamespace Erdos758
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üí/-- A cochromatic coloring of a simple graph $G$ with $k$ colors is a function
    39‚Üí$c : V \to \text{Fin}(k)$ such that each color class is either a clique (all pairs
    40‚Üíadjacent) or an independent set (no pairs adjacent). -/
    41‚Üídef IsCochromaticColoring {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) (k : ‚Ąē)
    42‚Üí    (c : V ‚Üí Fin k) : Prop :=
    43‚Üí  ‚ąÄ i : Fin k,
    44‚Üí    (‚ąÄ u v : V, c u = i ‚Üí c v = i ‚Üí u ‚Č† v ‚Üí G.Adj u v) ‚ą®
    45‚Üí    (‚ąÄ u v : V, c u = i ‚Üí c v = i ‚Üí u ‚Č† v ‚Üí ¬¨G.Adj u v)
    46‚Üí
    47‚Üí/-- The cochromatic number $\zeta(G)$: the minimum number of colors needed for a
    48‚Üícochromatic coloring of $G$. -/
    49‚Üínoncomputable def cochromaticNumber {V : Type*} [Fintype V]
    50‚Üí    (G : SimpleGraph V) : ‚Ąē :=
    51‚Üí  sInf {k : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÉ c : V ‚Üí Fin k, IsCochromaticColoring G k c}
    52‚Üí
    53‚Üí/-- $z(n)$: the maximum cochromatic number over all simple graphs on $n$
    54‚Üívertices. -/
    55‚Üínoncomputable def maxCochromaticNumber (n : ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē :=
    56‚Üí  sSup {k : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÉ G : SimpleGraph (Fin n), cochromaticNumber G = k}
    57‚Üí
    58‚Üí/--
    59‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 758 [ErGi93]:
    60‚Üí
    61‚ÜíIs it true that $z(12) = 4$, where $z(n)$ is the maximum cochromatic number
    62‚Üíover all graphs on $n$ vertices?
    63‚Üí
    64‚ÜíResolved: $z(12) = 4$ (confirmed computationally by Bhavik Mehta).
    65‚Üí-/
    66‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    67‚Üítheorem erdos_758 : answer(True) ‚ÜĒ maxCochromaticNumber 12 = 4 := by
    68‚Üí  sorry
    69‚Üí
    70‚Üíend Erdos758
    71‚Üí

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 759
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/759](https://www.erdosproblems.com/759)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíThe cochromatic number of $G$, denoted by $\zeta(G)$, is the minimum number of colours
    25‚Üíneeded to colour the vertices of $G$ such that each colour class induces either
    26‚Üía complete graph or an empty graph.
    27‚Üí
    28‚ÜíLet $z(S_n)$ be the maximum value of $\zeta(G)$ over all graphs $G$ which can be embedded
    29‚Üíon $S_n$, the orientable surface of genus $n$. Determine the growth rate of $z(S_n)$.
    30‚Üí
    31‚ÜíA problem of ErdŇĎs and Gimbel [ErGi93]. Gimbel [Gi86] proved that
    32‚Üí$$\sqrt{n} / \log n \ll z(S_n) \ll \sqrt{n}.$$
    33‚Üí
    34‚ÜíSolved by Gimbel and Thomassen [GiTh97], who proved
    35‚Üí$$z(S_n) \asymp \sqrt{n} / \log n.$$
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí[ErGi93] ErdŇĎs, P. and Gimbel, J., *Some problems and results in cochromatic theory*, 1993.
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üí[Gi86] Gimbel, J., *Some remarks on the cochromatic number of a graph*, 1986.
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üí[GiTh97] Gimbel, J. and Thomassen, C., *Coloring graphs with fixed genus and girth*, 1997.
    42‚Üí-/
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üíopen SimpleGraph Real
    45‚Üí
    46‚Üínamespace Erdos759
    47‚Üí
    48‚Üí/-- A colouring $c : V \to \operatorname{Fin}(k)$ is a cochromatic colouring of $G$ if every colour
    49‚Üíclass induces either a complete subgraph or an independent set. -/
    50‚Üídef IsCochromaticColouring {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) (k : ‚Ąē)
    51‚Üí    (c : V ‚Üí Fin k) : Prop :=
    52‚Üí  ‚ąÄ i : Fin k,
    53‚Üí    (‚ąÄ u v, c u = i ‚Üí c v = i ‚Üí u ‚Č† v ‚Üí G.Adj u v) ‚ą®
    54‚Üí    (‚ąÄ u v, c u = i ‚Üí c v = i ‚Üí u ‚Č† v ‚Üí ¬¨G.Adj u v)
    55‚Üí
    56‚Üí/-- The cochromatic number $\zeta(G)$ of a finite graph $G$: the minimum number of
    57‚Üícolours in a cochromatic colouring. -/
    58‚Üínoncomputable def cochromaticNumber {V : Type*} [Fintype V]
    59‚Üí    (G : SimpleGraph V) : ‚Ąē :=
    60‚Üí  sInf {k : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÉ c : V ‚Üí Fin k, IsCochromaticColouring G k c}
    61‚Üí
    62‚Üí/-- Embeddability of a finite simple graph on the orientable surface of genus $n$.
    63‚ÜíThis is axiomatized since surface topology is not available in Mathlib. -/
    64‚Üíopaque IsEmbeddableOnSurface {m : ‚Ąē} (_ : SimpleGraph (Fin m)) (_ : ‚Ąē) : Prop
    65‚Üí
    66‚Üí/-- $z(S_n)$: the maximum cochromatic number over all finite graphs embeddable
    67‚Üíon the orientable surface of genus $n$. -/
    68‚Üínoncomputable def maxCochromaticOnSurface (n : ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē :=
    69‚Üí  sSup {k : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÉ (m : ‚Ąē) (G : SimpleGraph (Fin m)),
    70‚Üí    IsEmbeddableOnSurface G n ‚ąß cochromaticNumber G = k}
    71‚Üí
    72‚Üí/--
    73‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 759, upper bound (Gimbel‚ÄďThomassen [GiTh97]):
    74‚Üí
    75‚ÜíThere exist $C > 0$ and $N_0$ such that for all $n \geq N_0$,
    76‚Üí$$z(S_n) \leq C \cdot \sqrt{n} / \log n.$$
    77‚Üí-/
    78‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    79‚Üítheorem erdos_759 :
    80‚Üí    ‚ąÉ C : ‚ĄĚ, C > 0 ‚ąß ‚ąÉ N‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, n ‚Č• N‚āÄ ‚Üí
    81‚Üí      (maxCochromaticOnSurface n : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č§
    82‚Üí        C * Real.sqrt (n : ‚ĄĚ) / Real.log (n : ‚ĄĚ) := by
    83‚Üí  sorry
    84‚Üí
    85‚Üí/--
    86‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 759, lower bound (Gimbel [Gi86]):
    87‚Üí
    88‚ÜíThere exist $C > 0$ and $N_0$ such that for all $n \geq N_0$,
    89‚Üí$$z(S_n) \geq C \cdot \sqrt{n} / \log n.$$
    90‚Üí-/

    94‚Üí      (maxCochromaticOnSurface n : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č•

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 760
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/760](https://www.erdosproblems.com/760)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíThe cochromatic number of $G$, denoted by $\zeta(G)$, is the minimum number of colours
    25‚Üíneeded to colour the vertices of $G$ such that each colour class induces either
    26‚Üía complete graph or an empty graph.
    27‚Üí
    28‚ÜíIf $G$ is a graph with chromatic number $\chi(G) = m$ then must $G$ contain a subgraph $H$
    29‚Üíwith $\zeta(H) \gg m / \log m$?
    30‚Üí
    31‚ÜíA problem of ErdŇĎs and Gimbel [ErGi93], who proved that there must exist a
    32‚Üísubgraph $H$ with $\zeta(H) \gg (m / \log m)^{1/2}$. The proposed bound would be best
    33‚Üípossible, as shown by taking $G$ to be a complete graph.
    34‚Üí
    35‚ÜíThe answer is yes, proved by Alon, Krivelevich, and Sudakov [AKS97].
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí[ErGi93] ErdŇĎs, P. and Gimbel, J.
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üí[AKS97] Alon, N., Krivelevich, M., and Sudakov, B.
    40‚Üí-/
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üíopen SimpleGraph Real
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üínamespace Erdos760
    45‚Üí
    46‚Üí/-- A colouring $c : V \to \operatorname{Fin} k$ is a cochromatic colouring of $G$ if every
    47‚Üícolour class induces either a complete subgraph or an independent set. -/
    48‚Üídef IsCochromaticColouring {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) (k : ‚Ąē)
    49‚Üí    (c : V ‚Üí Fin k) : Prop :=
    50‚Üí  ‚ąÄ i : Fin k,
    51‚Üí    (‚ąÄ u v, c u = i ‚Üí c v = i ‚Üí u ‚Č† v ‚Üí G.Adj u v) ‚ą®
    52‚Üí    (‚ąÄ u v, c u = i ‚Üí c v = i ‚Üí u ‚Č† v ‚Üí ¬¨G.Adj u v)
    53‚Üí
    54‚Üí/-- The cochromatic number $\zeta(G)$ of a finite graph $G$: the minimum number of
    55‚Üícolours in a cochromatic colouring. -/
    56‚Üínoncomputable def cochromaticNumber {V : Type*} [Fintype V]
    57‚Üí    (G : SimpleGraph V) : ‚Ąē :=
    58‚Üí  sInf {k : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÉ c : V ‚Üí Fin k, IsCochromaticColouring G k c}
    59‚Üí
    60‚Üí/--
    61‚Üí**ErdŇĎs Problem 760** (Proved, Alon‚ÄďKrivelevich‚ÄďSudakov [AKS97]):
    62‚Üí
    63‚ÜíThere exists a constant $C > 0$ such that for every graph $G$ with sufficiently
    64‚Üílarge chromatic number $m = \chi(G)$, there exists an induced subgraph $H$ of $G$ with
    65‚Üícochromatic number $\zeta(H) \geq C \cdot m / \log m$.
    66‚Üí-/
    67‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    68‚Üítheorem erdos_760 :
    69‚Üí    answer(True) ‚ÜĒ
    70‚Üí    ‚ąÉ C : ‚ĄĚ, C > 0 ‚ąß ‚ąÉ N‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ (n : ‚Ąē) (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)),
    71‚Üí      G.chromaticNumber.toNat ‚Č• N‚āÄ ‚Üí
    72‚Üí        ‚ąÉ (S : Finset (Fin n)),
    73‚Üí          (cochromaticNumber (G.induce (‚ÜĎS : Set (Fin n))) : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č•
    74‚Üí            C * (G.chromaticNumber.toNat : ‚ĄĚ) / Real.log (G.chromaticNumber.toNat : ‚ĄĚ) := by
    75‚Üí  sorry
    76‚Üí
    77‚Üíend Erdos760

    22‚Üí
    23‚ÜíThis file introduces dominating sets and related invariants.
    24‚Üí
    25‚ÜíMain definitions
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üí* `SimpleGraph.IsDominating`   : A set of vertices that dominates all vertices.
    28‚Üí* `SimpleGraph.IsNDominatingSet` : A dominating set with `n` vertices.
    29‚Üí* `SimpleGraph.dominationNumber` : The domination number of a graph.
    30‚Üí* `SimpleGraph.IsTotalDominating` : A total dominating set.
    31‚Üí* `SimpleGraph.IsTotalNDominatingSet` : A total dominating set with `n` vertices.
    32‚Üí* `SimpleGraph.totalDominationNumber` : The total domination number.

    34‚ÜíFuture work should extend this file with connected, independent, and power
    35‚Üívariants as well as domination-related lemmas.

    37‚Üí
    38‚Üínamespace SimpleGraph
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üívariable {őĪ : Type*} {G : SimpleGraph őĪ}
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üí/-! ### Dominating sets -/
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üí/-- A set `D` is a dominating set for `G` if every vertex of `G` is either in
    45‚Üí`D` or adjacent to a vertex of `D`. -/
    46‚Üídef IsDominating (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) (D : Set őĪ) : Prop :=
    47‚Üí  ‚ąÄ v, v ‚ąą D ‚ą® ‚ąÉ w ‚ąą D, G.Adj v w
    48‚Üí
    49‚Üí/-- An `n`-dominating set is a dominating set with `n` vertices. -/
    50‚Üí@[mk_iff]
    51‚Üístructure IsNDominatingSet (n : ‚Ąē) (D : Finset őĪ) : Prop where
    52‚Üí  isDominating : G.IsDominating D
    53‚Üí  card_eq : D.card = n
    54‚Üí
    55‚Üí/-! ### Domination number -/
    56‚Üí
    57‚Üí/-- The domination number of a graph `G` is the minimum size of a dominating
    58‚Üíset. It is `0` if there are no vertices. -/
    59‚Üínoncomputable def dominationNumber (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) : ‚Ąē :=
    60‚Üí  sInf {n | ‚ąÉ D : Finset őĪ, G.IsNDominatingSet n D}
    61‚Üí
    62‚Üí/-! ### Total domination -/
    63‚Üí
    64‚Üí/-- A set `D` is a total dominating set if every vertex is adjacent to a vertex
    65‚Üíin `D`. -/
    66‚Üídef IsTotalDominating (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) (D : Set őĪ) : Prop :=
    67‚Üí  ‚ąÄ v, ‚ąÉ w ‚ąą D, G.Adj v w
    68‚Üí
    69‚Üí/-- An `n`-total dominating set is a total dominating set with `n` vertices. -/
    70‚Üí@[mk_iff]
    71‚Üístructure IsTotalNDominatingSet (n : ‚Ąē) (D : Finset őĪ) : Prop where
    72‚Üí  isTotalDominating : G.IsTotalDominating D
    73‚Üí  card_eq : D.card = n
    74‚Üí
    75‚Üí/-- The total domination number of `G`. -/
    76‚Üínoncomputable def totalDominationNumber (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) : ‚Ąē :=
    77‚Üí  sInf {n | ‚ąÉ D : Finset őĪ, G.IsTotalNDominatingSet n D}
    78‚Üí
    79‚Üí/-! ### Connected domination -/
    80‚Üí
    81‚Üí/-- A set is a connected dominating set if it is dominating and induces a
    82‚Üíconnected subgraph. -/
    83‚Üídef IsConnectedDominating (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) (D : Set őĪ) : Prop :=
    84‚Üí  G.IsDominating D ‚ąß (G.induce D).Connected
    85‚Üí
    86‚Üí/-- The connected domination number of `G`. -/
    87‚Üínoncomputable def connectedDominationNumber (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) : ‚Ąē :=
    88‚Üí  sInf {n | ‚ąÉ D : Finset őĪ, G.IsConnectedDominating (D : Set őĪ) ‚ąß D.card = n}
    89‚Üí
    90‚Üí/-! ### Independent domination -/
    91‚Üí
    92‚Üídef IsIndepDominating (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) (D : Set őĪ) : Prop :=
    93‚Üí  G.IsIndepSet D ‚ąß G.IsDominating D
    94‚Üí
    95‚Üí@[mk_iff]
    96‚Üístructure IsNIndepDominatingSet (n : ‚Ąē) (D : Finset őĪ) : Prop where
    97‚Üí  isIndep : G.IsIndepSet D
    98‚Üí  isDominating : G.IsDominating D
    99‚Üí  card_eq : D.card = n
   100‚Üí
   101‚Üínoncomputable def indepDominationNumber (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) : ‚Ąē :=
   102‚Üí  sInf {n | ‚ąÉ D : Finset őĪ, G.IsNIndepDominatingSet n D}
   103‚Üí
   104‚Üí/-! ### Vertex and edge covers -/
   105‚Üí
   106‚Üí/-- A set of edges is an edge cover if every vertex is incident to some edge in it. -/
   107‚Üídef IsEdgeCover (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) (M : Set (Sym2 őĪ)) : Prop :=
   108‚Üí  M ‚äÜ G.edgeSet ‚ąß ‚ąÄ v, ‚ąÉ e ‚ąą M, v ‚ąą e
   109‚Üí

   112‚Üí  sInf {n | ‚ąÉ M : Finset (Sym2 őĪ), G.IsEdgeCover (M : Set (Sym2 őĪ)) ‚ąß M.card = n}
   113‚Üí

   118‚Üídef IsEdgeDominating (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) (M : Set (Sym2 őĪ)) : Prop :=
   119‚Üí  ‚ąÄ ‚¶Ée‚¶Ą, e ‚ąą G.edgeSet ‚Üí e ‚ąą M ‚ą® ‚ąÉ e' ‚ąą M, edgesAdjacent e e'
   120‚Üí
   121‚Üí@[mk_iff]
   122‚Üístructure IsNEdgeDominatingSet (n : ‚Ąē) (M : Finset (Sym2 őĪ)) : Prop where
   123‚Üí  isDominating : G.IsEdgeDominating (M : Set (Sym2 őĪ))

   126‚Üínoncomputable def edgeDominationNumber (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) : ‚Ąē :=
   127‚Üí  sInf {n | ‚ąÉ M : Finset (Sym2 őĪ), G.IsNEdgeDominatingSet n M}

   129‚Üíend SimpleGraph

Let $G$ be a graph with girth $> 4$. Can the edges of $G$ always be oriented so that the
resulting directed graph is acyclic, and reversing any single edge also leaves it acyclic?
Disproved by NeŇ°etŇôil and R√∂dl.

[Er71] ErdŇĎs, P., *Some unsolved problems in graph theory and combinatorial analysis*, 1971.

[Er76b] ErdŇĎs, P., *Problems and results in graph theory*, 1976.

[NeRo78b] NeŇ°etŇôil, J. and R√∂dl, V., *A short proof of the existence of highly chromatic
hypergraphs without short cycles*, 1978.

/-- An orientation of a simple graph $G$: a relation `dir` on $V$ such that for each
edge $\{u, v\}$, exactly one of `dir u v` or `dir v u` holds, and `dir` only
relates adjacent vertices. -/
structure Orientation (G : SimpleGraph V) where

  dir_of_adj : ‚ąÄ u v, G.Adj u v ‚Üí dir u v ‚ą® dir v u

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/1007](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1007)

The dimension of a graph $G$ is the minimal $n$ such that $G$ can be embedded in
$\mathbb{R}^n$ such that every edge of $G$ is a unit line segment (adjacent vertices are at
Euclidean distance exactly $1$, and non-adjacent vertices are not). This notion
was defined by ErdŇĎs, Harary, and Tutte.

[So09e] Soifer, A., *The Mathematical Coloring Book*, Springer, 2009.

[Ho13] House, J., *Graphs of dimension 4*, 2013.

[ChNo16] Chaffee, J. and Noble, S., *An alternative proof*, 2016.

open SimpleGraph

namespace Erdos1007

/-- A unit-distance representation of a simple graph $G$ in $\mathbb{R}^n$: an injective map
from vertices to Euclidean $n$-space such that adjacency is equivalent to
distance exactly $1$. -/
def IsUnitDistRep {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) (n : ‚Ąē)
    (f : V ‚Üí EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin n)) : Prop :=
  Function.Injective f ‚ąß
  ‚ąÄ u v, G.Adj u v ‚ÜĒ dist (f u) (f v) = 1

/-- A graph admits a unit-distance representation in $\mathbb{R}^n$. -/
def HasUnitDistRep {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) (n : ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
  ‚ąÉ f : V ‚Üí EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin n), IsUnitDistRep G n f

   174‚Üí/-- The multiset of degrees of a graph. -/
   175‚Üídef degreeMultiset (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : Multiset ‚Ąē :=
   176‚Üí  Finset.univ.val.map fun v => G.degree v
   177‚Üí
   178‚Üí/-- The annihilation number of a graph. This is the largest number of degrees that can be added
   179‚Üítogether without going over the total number of edges of that graph. -/
   180‚Üídef annihilationNumber (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚Ąē :=
   181‚Üí  -- Calculate the limit: The number of edges (Sum of degrees / 2)
   182‚Üí  letI limit := G.edgeFinset.card
   183‚Üí
   184‚Üí  -- The set of all multisets of degrees that sum to less than or equal to `limit`
   185‚Üí  Finset.Iic (degreeMultiset G)
   186‚Üí    |>.filter (fun S ‚Ü¶ Multiset.sum S ‚Č§ limit)
   187‚Üí    |>.sup Multiset.card
   188‚Üí
   189‚Üí/--
   190‚ÜíComputes the annihilation number of a graph G.
   191‚Üí
   192‚ÜíIt calculates the degree sequence, sorts it ascendingly, and finds the largest prefix length 'k'
   193‚Üí(where `0 ‚Č§ k ‚Č§ |V(G)|`) such that the sum of the prefix is less than or equal to the sum of the corresponding suffix.
   194‚Üí-/
   195‚Üínoncomputable def annihilationNumber' (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚Ąē :=
   196‚Üí  -- 1. Get the degree sequence sorted in ascending order.
   197‚Üí  -- G.degree_list returns the list of degrees.
   198‚Üí  letI degrees := (Finset.univ.image fun v => G.degree v).sort (¬∑ ‚Č§ ¬∑)
   199‚Üí
   200‚Üí  -- 2. Define the condition for the annihilation number.
   201‚Üí  -- k represents the number of smallest degrees considered (the length of the prefix).
   202‚Üí  letI condition (k : ‚Ąē) : Bool := (degrees.take k).sum ‚Č§ (degrees.drop k).sum
   203‚Üí
   204‚Üí  -- 3. Find the maximum k in {0, ..., n} satisfying the condition.
   205‚Üí  -- List.range (n + 1) generates the list [0, 1, ..., n].
   206‚Üí  letI candidates := (List.range (Fintype.card őĪ + 1)).filter condition
   207‚Üí
   208‚Üí  -- 4. Return the maximum candidate.
   209‚Üí  -- The list of candidates is guaranteed to be non-empty because k=0 always satisfies
   210‚Üí  -- the condition (0 ‚Č§ sum of all degrees).
   211‚Üí  candidates.getLast!
   212‚Üí
   213‚Üíset_option linter.unusedSectionVars false in
   214‚Üí-- TODO(Paul-Lez): debug the issue with the unused variable linter...
   215‚Üíproof_wanted annihilationNumberEq (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) [DecidableRel G.Adj] :
   216‚Üí    annihilationNumber G = annihilationNumber' G
   217‚Üí
   218‚Üí/-!
   219‚Üí## Residue
   220‚ÜíThe residue of a graph is the number of zeros remaining after iteratively applying the Havel-Hakimi
   221‚Üíalgorithm to the degree sequence until all remaining degrees are zero.
   222‚Üí-/
   223‚Üí
   224‚Üí/--
   225‚ÜíHelper function: Performs one step of the Havel-Hakimi reduction on a degree sequence.
   226‚ÜíAssumes the input list `s` is sorted descending.
   227‚ÜíRemoves the first element `d`, decrements the next `d` elements by 1, and re-sorts the list descending.
   228‚Üí
   229‚ÜíNote: when `s` is the list of vertices arising from a simple graph, if the first index is `s` then
   230‚Üíthe degree list always has length at least `s+1` so this makes sense.
   231‚Üí-/
   232‚Üíprivate def havelHakimiStep (s : List ‚Ąē) : List ‚Ąē :=
   233‚Üí  match s with
   234‚Üí  | [] => []
   235‚Üí  | d :: rest =>
   236‚Üí    -- Split the rest into the part to decrement (first d elements) and the remaining part.
   237‚Üí    let (to_decrement, remaining) := rest.splitAt d
   238‚Üí    -- Decrement the elements
   239‚Üí    let decremented := to_decrement.map (¬∑ - 1)
   240‚Üí    -- Combine and re-sort descending.
   241‚Üí    (decremented ++ remaining).mergeSort (¬∑ ‚Č• ¬∑)
   242‚Üí
   243‚Üí/--
   244‚ÜíAuxiliary function to calculate the residue recursively.
   245‚ÜíApplies Havel-Hakimi steps until the sequence consists only of zeros or is empty.
   246‚Üí-/
   247‚Üíprivate partial def residueAux : List ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē
   248‚Üí  | [] => 0        -- Empty sequence, residue is 0.
   249‚Üí  | 0 :: s => 1 + s.length -- If the largest degree is 0 (and the list is sorted), all are 0.
   250‚Üí  | s => residueAux (havelHakimiStep s) -- Apply one reduction step and recurse.
   251‚Üí
   252‚Üí/--
   253‚ÜíComputes the residue of a graph G, ,i.e. the number of zeros remaining after iteratively applying the Havel-Hakimi
   254‚Üíalgorithm to the degree sequence until all remaining degrees are zero.
   255‚ÜíStarts with the descending degree sequence and applies the Havel-Hakimi process.
   256‚Üí-/
   257‚Üínoncomputable def residue (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚Ąē :=
   258‚Üí  -- Get the degree sequence sorted in descending order and apply `residueAux`.
   259‚Üí  residueAux ((Finset.univ.image fun v => G.degree v).sort (¬∑ ‚Č• ¬∑))
   260‚Üí
   261‚Üí/--
   262‚ÜíFractional alpha. This is defined as
   263‚Üí$$
   264‚Üí\max_x \sum_{v \in V} x_v
   265‚Üí$$
   266‚Üísubject to $0 \le x_v \le 1$ for all $v \in V$ and
   267‚Üí$x_u + x_v \le 1$ whenever $(u, v)$ are connected by an edge.
   268‚Üí-/
   269‚Üínoncomputable def fractionalAlpha (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚ĄĚ :=
   270‚Üí  sSup {(‚ąĎ i, x i) | (x : őĪ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) (_ : ‚ąÄ v, x v ‚ąą Set.Icc 0 1)
   271‚Üí    (_ : ‚ąÄ u v, G.Adj u v ‚Üí x u + x v ‚Č§ 1)}
   272‚Üí
   273‚Üí/--
   274‚ÜíLov√°sz Theta Function (ŌĎ(G))
   275‚ÜíThe Lov√°sz theta function is defined as:
   276‚ÜíŌĎ(G) = min őĽ_max(A)
   277‚Üíwhere the minimum is taken over all symmetric matrices A such that:
   278‚Üí
   279‚ÜíA_ij = 1 for all i = j (diagonal entries are 1)
   280‚ÜíA_ij = 0 for all {i,j} ‚ąą E (entries corresponding to edges are 0)
   281‚ÜíA is positive semidefinite
   282‚Üí
   283‚ÜíHere őĽ_max(A) denotes the maximum eigenvalue of A.
   284‚Üí-/
   285‚Üínoncomputable def lovaczThetaFunction
   286‚Üí    (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚ĄĚ :=
   287‚Üí  sInf {(Matrix.IsHermitian.maxEigenvalue hA) | (A : Matrix őĪ őĪ ‚ĄĚ) (hA : A.IsHermitian)
   288‚Üí      (_ : ‚ąÄ i, A i i = 1) (_ : ‚ąÄ i j, G.Adj i j ‚Üí A i j = 0)}
   289‚Üí
   290‚Üí/--
   291‚ÜíGiven a simple graph `G` with adjacency matrix `A`, this is the number `n‚āÄ + min n‚āä n‚āč` where:
   292‚Üí- `n‚āÄ` is the multiplicity of zero as an eigenvalue of `A`
   293‚Üí- `n‚āä` is the number of positive eigenvalues of `A` (counting multiplicities)
   294‚Üí- `n‚āč` is the number of negative eigenvalues of `A` (counting multiplicities)
   295‚Üí-/
   296‚Üínoncomputable def cvetkovic (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚Ąē :=
   297‚Üí  letI A : Matrix őĪ őĪ ‚ĄĚ := G.adjMatrix ‚ĄĚ
   298‚Üí  letI spectrum : Multiset ‚ĄĚ := (Matrix.charpoly A).roots
   299‚Üí  letI positive_count : ‚Ąē := spectrum.countP (fun x => 0 < x)
   300‚Üí  letI negative_count : ‚Ąē := spectrum.countP (fun x => 0 > x)
   301‚Üí  letI zero_count : ‚Ąē := spectrum.countP (fun x => 0 = x)
   302‚Üí  zero_count + min positive_count negative_count
   303‚Üí
   304‚Üíend SimpleGraph
   305‚Üí
